name: review
context: Request, submit, and manage pull request code reviews via the GitHub API or gh pr review
info: Manage the code review process for pull requests: request reviewers, submit reviews with APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/COMMENT events, add inline comments on specific file lines, and dismiss stale reviews. Reviews integrate with branch protection rules in [[github/repos/settings]] to gate merges on required approvals. Use `octokit.pulls.createReview()` or `gh pr review`. See [[github/auth]] for authentication and [[github/pulls/merge]] for merging after approval.
body:
# Pull Request Reviews

Code reviews are the quality gate for pull requests. Reviewers approve changes, request modifications, or leave comments.

## gh CLI

```bash
# Approve a PR
gh pr review 42 --approve --body "LGTM! Clean implementation."

# Request changes
gh pr review 42 --request-changes --body "Please add error handling for the auth middleware."

# Leave a comment (without approve/reject)
gh pr review 42 --comment --body "Nice approach, a few suggestions below."

# Request specific reviewers
gh pr edit 42 --add-reviewer reviewer-1,reviewer-2
gh pr edit 42 --add-reviewer org/team-name
```

## REST API

```javascript
import { Octokit } from "@octokit/rest";
const octokit = new Octokit({ auth: process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN });

// Request reviewers
await octokit.pulls.requestReviewers({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  pull_number: 42,
  reviewers: ["reviewer-1", "reviewer-2"],
  team_reviewers: ["security-team"],
});

// Submit an approval
await octokit.pulls.createReview({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  pull_number: 42,
  event: "APPROVE",
  body: "LGTM! Code looks clean and tests are comprehensive.",
});

// Request changes with inline comments
await octokit.pulls.createReview({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  pull_number: 42,
  event: "REQUEST_CHANGES",
  body: "A few issues need addressing before merge.",
  comments: [
    {
      path: "src/auth/middleware.js",
      position: 15,  // diff position (not line number)
      body: "This needs try/catch — JWT verification can throw.",
    },
    {
      path: "src/auth/middleware.js",
      line: 28,       // line number in the file
      side: "RIGHT",  // RIGHT = new code, LEFT = old code
      body: "Consider using `token.exp` instead of manual timestamp comparison.",
    },
  ],
});

// List reviews on a PR
const { data: reviews } = await octokit.pulls.listReviews({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  pull_number: 42,
});
reviews.forEach(r => console.log(r.user.login, r.state));

// Dismiss a review
await octokit.pulls.dismissReview({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  pull_number: 42,
  review_id: 123456,
  message: "Dismissing stale review — code has been updated.",
});
```

```bash
# REST via curl — submit approval
curl -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" \
  -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
  https://api.github.com/repos/owner/repo/pulls/42/reviews \
  -d '{"event": "APPROVE", "body": "LGTM"}'
```

## Review Events

| Event | Meaning | Effect |
|-------|---------|--------|
| `APPROVE` | Approve the PR | Counts toward required approvals |
| `REQUEST_CHANGES` | Request modifications | Blocks merge until resolved |
| `COMMENT` | General feedback | No approval/rejection status |
| `PENDING` | In-progress review | Not submitted yet |

## Inline Comments

Two positioning modes for inline comments:

- **`position`** — offset in the diff hunk (legacy, 1-based from the first `@@` line)
- **`line` + `side`** — absolute line number in the file. `side: "RIGHT"` targets new code, `side: "LEFT"` targets removed code. Preferred over `position`.

## Branch Protection Integration

Branch protection rules in [[github/repos/settings]] can require:
- Minimum number of approving reviews (e.g., 1 or 2)
- Dismissal of stale reviews when new commits are pushed
- Require review from code owners (CODEOWNERS file)
- Restrict who can dismiss reviews

sections.title: Requesting reviewers
sections.body: Use `octokit.pulls.requestReviewers()` with `reviewers` (individual usernames) and `team_reviewers` (team slugs). Or use `gh pr edit 42 --add-reviewer user1,org/team`. GitHub notifies requested reviewers. CODEOWNERS files can auto-assign reviewers based on file paths.

sections.title: Submitting reviews
sections.body: Use `octokit.pulls.createReview()` with `event` (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, COMMENT) and optional `body` and `comments` array. The `gh pr review` CLI provides `--approve`, `--request-changes`, and `--comment` flags. Reviews with REQUEST_CHANGES block merge on protected branches until resolved.

sections.title: Inline comments
sections.body: Add file-specific comments in the `comments` array of `createReview()`. Use `line` + `side` (preferred) or `position` (legacy diff offset) to target specific lines. `side: RIGHT` targets new code, `LEFT` targets removed code. Multi-line comments use `start_line` + `start_side` in addition to `line` + `side`.

sections.title: Dismissing reviews
sections.body: Dismiss stale reviews with `octokit.pulls.dismissReview()` providing `review_id` and `message`. Branch protection can auto-dismiss reviews when new commits are pushed. Only users with write access (or admins) can dismiss reviews. Dismissal leaves an audit trail.

Branch protection rules require review approvals
